target.name: Rain Industries
target.founder: Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, Maxwell Brodie

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rain Industries is a seed-stage drone and autonomous systems company founded by Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, and Maxwell Brodie that has raised $14.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of seven private companies in Drones & Autonomous, including peers such as Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron; no public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using the $14.7M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $37M. The peer-median approach is not applicable: while four same-sector peers exist, insufficient datapoints met the narrow filters for same-stage and adjacent-stage comparisons (fewer than three companies in each cohort), making a compressed peer median unreliable.

The triangulated range is $31M to $42M, with a median of $37M, derived from the single applicable method. Key uncertainties center on the absence of same-stage peer benchmarks within the Drones & Autonomous sector and the reliance on a generalized stage-step multiplier rather than company-specific metrics. This valuation should be validated through primary diligence on unit economics, technology differentiation, and path to revenue.
